name: Stargate — Abilene Flagship
status: Partially Operational — 2 of 8 buildings live since Sep 2025, remaining 6 by mid-2026
details: Flagship campus of the $500B Stargate initiative. 4 million sq ft on 1,100 acres. Over 450,000 NVIDIA GB200 GPUs planned. 6,400+ construction workers on site. Developed by Crusoe Energy on ERCOT grid with renewables.

name: Stargate — Frontier Campus (Shackelford County)
status: Under Construction — first building expected H2 2026
details: 10 buildings on 700 acres, 3.7 million sq ft. Powered by onsite natural gas microgrid (Jenbacher engines). Closed-loop water cooling. Over 5,000 construction jobs, 1,600 permanent when operational.

name: Stargate — Doña Ana County
location: Doña Ana County, New Mexico, USA
details: 4 buildings on 1,400 acres. Developed by STACK Infrastructure and Orion Digital. Closed-loop cooling. $50M committed to local water/wastewater systems. $384M projected annual economic output.

name: Stargate — Lordstown
details: SoftBank-led campus using advanced data center design. Part of the Stargate expansion beyond Texas. Expected online 2026.

name: Stargate — Milam County
details: SB Energy (SoftBank's power subsidiary) building and operating. OpenAI and SoftBank each invested $500M in SB Energy. Accelerated power infrastructure with new substations.

name: xAI Colossus
status: Expanding — Colossus 1 operational, Colossus 2 adding capacity, Building 3 conversion starting Q1 2026
details: World's largest single-site AI training installation. 555,000+ NVIDIA GPUs (H100/H200/GB200). Three buildings. On-site gas-fired power plant under construction across Mississippi border. Built in record 122 days initially. Trains Grok AI models.

name: Amazon Project Rainier
status: Partially Operational — 7 of 30 buildings live, completion ~2027
details: 1,200 acres dedicated to training/running Anthropic Claude models. Largest known deployment of non-NVIDIA computing (500,000+ AWS Trainium2 chips). Will scale to 1 million chips. Natural gas power supplement being acquired.

name: Meta Hyperion
status: Under Construction — 3,700+ workers on site, Phase 2 land acquired
details: Originally 2,250 acres, expanded with ~1,400 additional acres. 4M+ sq ft. Entergy building 3 new natural gas plants ($3B) nearby. Joint venture with Blue Owl Capital targeting $27B total development. Trump stated Meta plans $50B total spend.

name: Meta Prometheus Campus
status: Under Construction — tent-style rapid-build structures, anticipated online 2026
details: Expanding existing campus with revolutionary tent-style AI buildings (hurricane-proof, deployable in 4-7 months vs 14-20 months). Nuclear power deals signed with Vistra (Perry/Davis-Besse plants). Natural gas via Williams pipelines.

name: Meta Indiana Data Center
details: Meta's latest $10B data center to boost AI capabilities. Construction announced February 2026. Part of Meta's $600B U.S. infrastructure commitment through 2028.

name: Microsoft Fairwater — Wisconsin
status: Under Construction — first building online early 2026, 15 more approved
details: World's most powerful AI datacenter. Two-story design with NVIDIA GB200/GB300 GPUs. Advanced liquid cooling (near-zero water). Connected to Atlanta via dedicated AI WAN fiber network forming 'AI Superfactory'. 9M+ sq ft proposed.

name: Microsoft Fairwater 2 — Atlanta
details: Two-story AI datacenter in South Fulton County. Connected to Wisconsin Fairwater via 120,000 miles of dedicated fiber (AI WAN). NVIDIA Blackwell GPUs. Closed-loop cooling. Part of world's first 'AI Superfactory' network. Powers OpenAI, Microsoft AI, Copilot.

name: Google — Armstrong County, TX
details: One of three new Google data centers in Texas (part of $40B investment). Texas Panhandle location. Workforce development program training 1,700 apprentices by 2030.

name: Google — Haskell County, TX (x2)
details: Two data centers in Haskell County. One paired with a new 600MW solar + battery storage plant. Near Abilene in West Texas. Part of Google's massive Texas build.

name: Google — West Memphis, AR
details: First Google data center in Arkansas. 1,000+ acres. Includes 600MW solar project + 350MW battery storage. $25M Energy Impact Fund for low-income residents. $1.1B net benefits over contract life to Entergy ratepayers.

name: Amazon — Louisiana Campuses
status: Construction Imminent — expected to begin within weeks (as of Feb 2026)
details: Multi-site campus across Caddo and Bossier Parishes. Amazon's first data center in Louisiana. Developed with STACK Infrastructure. 540 on-site jobs + 1,700 community jobs. 1,500 construction jobs.

name: Amazon — Ohio Expansion
status: Ongoing Expansion — new sites being finalized across Ohio
details: Largest single private-sector investment in Ohio history (2nd only to Intel). Expanding beyond Central Ohio. Cloud computing and AI/ML workloads. Contingent on long-term energy service agreements.

name: Anthropic / Fluidstack — Texas
details: Part of Anthropic's $50B U.S. AI infrastructure deal with Fluidstack. Custom-built for Anthropic's Claude AI workloads. ~800 permanent jobs ($144K avg salary) and 2,400 construction jobs across both TX and NY sites.

name: Anthropic / Fluidstack — New York
details: Second Anthropic data center location. Built by Fluidstack with TeraWulf involvement (Google-backed). Likely on the shore of Lake Ontario. Custom AI workload optimization.

name: CoreWeave — Kenilworth, NJ
status: Under Construction — started Sep 2025, expected operational early 2027
details: 392,600 sq ft (108K new + 284K retrofit of former Merck campus). First NJ data center for CoreWeave. $250M state tax credit. NVIDIA-backed. Part of 5GW buildout plan by 2030.

name: CoreWeave / Applied Digital — Ellendale, ND
status: Under Construction — first 100MW ready Q4 2025, 150MW building mid-2026
details: Purpose-built for next-gen AI/HPC workloads. Two 15-year lease agreements. Campus engineered for high-density compute. Option for additional 150MW third building (2027).

name: Nebius AI Factory — Independence, MO
details: Dutch company's U.S. hyperscale anchor. 398 acres, up to 10 buildings, 2.5M sq ft. Closed-loop water cooling. Separate private power plant. $6.6B in tax breaks. $651M in PILOT fees over 20 years. 8th hyperscale in KC metro.

name: Apple AI Server Factory
status: Operational — first test units July 2025, mass production 2026
details: 250,000 sq ft factory producing AI servers for Private Cloud Compute. Apple Silicon-powered servers. Previously manufactured outside U.S. Part of Apple's broader data center expansion in Iowa, Nevada, Oregon, and North Carolina.

name: Nscale / Microsoft / Aker — Narvik, Norway
details: Arctic Circle location chosen for hydropower and cool climate. 52,000 NVIDIA GPUs planned. Serves Microsoft, OpenAI, and other clients. 100% renewable energy. Part of Europe's largest AI infrastructure push.

name: Nscale — Loughton, UK
details: UK's largest planned AI supercomputer. 23,000+ NVIDIA GPUs. Built for Microsoft and OpenAI workloads. Part of Nscale's multi-site European GPU deployment.

name: Google — Dietzenbach & Hanau, Germany
status: Under Construction — new Dietzenbach facility + Hanau expansion
details: New data center in Dietzenbach + expansion of existing Hanau campus. Excess heat recovery for district heating. Powered by onshore wind, solar, and Ørsted offshore wind. First heat recovery project in Germany for Google.

name: Nscale — Start Campus, Sines, Portugal
status: Under Development — GPU deployment starting Q1 2026
details: Europe's largest colocation campus. 12,600 NVIDIA GPUs deploying for Microsoft. Adjacent to decommissioned power plant. Access to seawater cooling infrastructure. Strategic position connecting Europe, Africa, Americas.

name: Nscale / Microsoft — Texas
status: Under Development — 104,000 GPUs deploying over 12-18 months
details: Nscale's largest U.S. site. Leased from Ionic Digital. 104,000 NVIDIA GB300 GPUs for Microsoft. Largest single GPU deployment in the Nscale-Microsoft deal.

name: UAE-US AI Campus — Abu Dhabi
details: Largest AI facility planned outside the US. 10 square miles. Phase 1 uses NVIDIA Grace Blackwell GB300. AWS, Google, Meta, Microsoft, xAI in discussions. Built by G42. Microsoft investing $15.2B in UAE (2023-2029).

name: NEOM / DataVolt AI Data Center
details: Part of Saudi Arabia's Vision 2030. Located in NEOM mega-city project near Red Sea. Intended to serve European AI developers. 30% cheaper than US operations. Humain (state-backed) pursuing $77B strategy for 1.9 GW by 2030.

name: Groq / Aramco Digital — Saudi Arabia
details: World's largest planned AI inference data center. Groq's specialized LPU (Language Processing Unit) chips optimized for inference. Partnership with Aramco Digital. Announced at LEAP 2025 tech conference.

name: OpenAI / NEXTDC — Sydney, Australia
details: OpenAI's major Asia-Pacific expansion. 258,000 sq meter site at NEXTDC's S7 campus, 45km west of Sydney. Serves CBA, Wesfarmers and other clients. Renewable energy PPAs. Water-free cooling technology.

name: AWS — Brandenburg, Germany
details: Additional AI-optimized capacity with liquid cooling and on-site batteries added to existing Brandenburg campus.

name: Google — Groningen, Netherlands
status: Under Expansion — 15-year offshore wind PPA signed
details: Scaling existing Groningen site to 250 MW. Backed by 15-year offshore wind power purchase agreement. Part of Google's European AI infrastructure push.

name: Microsoft — Dataland, North Rhine-Westphalia, Germany
details: Upgrading to Tier 4 power and integrating district heating connections. Part of Microsoft's European cloud expansion for AI workloads.

name: STACK Infrastructure — Johor, Malaysia
details: Johor is Southeast Asia's hyperscale hub (66% of regional capacity under construction). Near Singapore for low-latency. Part of growing Johor-Batam-Thailand AI corridor. Over 42 data center projects approved in Johor.

name: xAI — Atlanta Data Center
details: Second xAI mega data center. Financed through $700M deal with Develop Fulton. ~12,448 NVIDIA GPUs (H100). Supports Grok AI training. $241M estimated economic impact for the region.
